Merge PDF

Combine multiple PDF files into one document. Upload, reorder, and merge PDFs entirely in your browser. Your files are never uploaded to any server.

No Signup
Browser-based
Free to use
Privacy-first
Files stay on your device. Not uploaded anywhere.

Drop PDF files here or click to choose

Select multiple PDF files to merge

Max 100MB per file

Advertisement

Ad space reserved for future

Advertisement space

Ad space reserved for future

Merge PDFs Without Uploading

This tool merges your PDF files entirely in your browser. No file is sent to any server. This makes it safe for combining sensitive documents such as salary slips, offer letters, ID proofs, or financial statements.

Common Use Cases

  • Combining multiple salary slips into one PDF for a loan application
  • Merging an offer letter, ID proof and address proof for KYC
  • Combining multiple marksheets or certificates for college applications
  • Joining chapter-wise PDFs into a single study material document
  • Merging invoices for an accounting or audit document set

How to Merge PDFs

  1. Upload all the PDF files you want to merge
  2. Use the arrows to set the correct order of the PDFs
  3. Click "Merge PDFs & Download" — the merged PDF downloads automatically

Limitations

  • Password-protected PDFs cannot be merged — remove the password first
  • Very large files (combined over 100MB) may be slow on older devices or slow connections
  • Some corrupted or non-standard PDF files may fail — use a standard PDF creator or printer driver to recreate the file if needed

Merge PDF — FAQs

Practical guidance for Merge PDF

Combine multiple PDF files into one. Upload, reorder and merge PDFs entirely in your browser. PDF tools are useful when a portal expects one clean upload, a smaller document, or pages in a specific order. JaldiDocs is designed for everyday Indian document workflows where a small mismatch in size, format, page order, or file weight can cause an upload to fail.

Before you use the tool

  • Keep the original file unchanged so you can restart if a form has a different requirement.
  • Check whether the portal asks for pixels, millimetres, page size, file format, or maximum KB/MB.
  • Use a clear file name without special symbols before uploading to government, exam, tax, or business portals.

After downloading

  • Keep the final PDF name simple, using letters, numbers, and hyphens.
  • Open the downloaded PDF once before upload to confirm page order and readability.
  • If a portal gives a size limit, compress or split the file before submitting.
  • Open the final file once on your device and confirm it is readable before submitting it anywhere.

Privacy and accuracy note

The tool runs in your browser wherever possible, which means your file is processed on your own device instead of being uploaded to JaldiDocs. Official requirements can still change, so always compare the output with the latest instructions from the website or organization where you plan to submit it.
